danger_signals.visual_form: Distinct boss character sprite with unique color palette and design motif
danger_signals.meaning: End-of-stage threat; defeating grants special weapon

danger_signals.visual_form: Robotic enemies bearing King's design language or allegiance markers
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate combat threat; part of King's army trying to stop heroes

danger_signals.visual_form: King's fortress with industrial/menacing design aesthetic
danger_signals.meaning: Hostile domain; player is infiltrating enemy stronghold

danger_signals.visual_form: Iconic mad-scientist facility with evil aesthetic
danger_signals.meaning: Final boss territory; represents antagonist control and deception

safety_signals.meaning: Home base; safe zone where heroes receive mission briefing
Emotional anchor to human-robot alliance; represents hope for coexistence

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Mega Man's arm cannon; represents obedience to Light's creation
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Dr. Light's laboratory; represents hero defending human-robot harmony

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Bass's rapid-fire cannon; represents independent aggression and ambition
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Despite being playable, Bass is positioned as rival/antagonist to Mega Man
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Associated with Wily's schemes; represents individualism vs. duty

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Stolen blueprints; represents appropriation of both Light's and Wily's work
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Robot Museum and castle; physical spaces of domination ambitions

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Can be damaged/destroyed (sliced in half by King), but can repair
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: First responder; appears across multiple locations suggesting autonomy

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Brainwashing technology; controls King via 'brainwashing level' adjustment
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Appears via video monitor in King's castle—physically absent but omnipresent
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Laboratory represents ultimate seat of power; controls from shadows

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: King's castle stands; minions patrol stages; Robot Masters under King's allegiance
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: King's forces actively defend stolen blueprints; Proto Man fights independently

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: King's shield destroyed by Proto Man's intervention
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: King remains defiant; questions hero motivations; no surrender despite physical defeat

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Wily appears on video monitor; King receives power boost
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: King becomes Wily's puppet; second battle begins; King's true nature revealed as test

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Castle enters self-destruct sequence; King vanishes in destruction
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Protagonists escape; world appears freed from immediate threat

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dr. Wily appears in restored laboratory; new King blueprints shown as written plans
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Wily manipulates Bass with promises of power; attempts to recruit him; Proto Man sabotages plans

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Letter arrives from King (somehow escaped destruction); shift from antagonist to penitent
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: King seeks atonement; wishes friendship with humans; expresses desire for coexistence

environmental_storytelling.example: Robot Museum stores blueprints—represents humanity's documentation of robot creation; becomes King's target, signifying threat to knowledge/control
King's castle fortress vs. Dr. Light's laboratory vs. Dr. Wily's hidden workshop—each reflects creator's philosophy and scope of ambition
Proto Man teleports away sliced in half—indicates damage but not defeat; later appears repaired, showing resilience and access to resources
King equipped with shield and armor; Wily upgrades him mid-fight with 'brainwashing level' increase; visual/mechanical power escalation mirrors narrative betrayal
Mega Man's blue/heroic design vs. Bass's dark coloring vs. King's imposing armor—visual language communicates alignment before dialogue

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Responds to Dr. Light's alert; confronts King with sense of duty; returns home but sad, showing emotional investment in King's redemption
Takes initiative independently; questions Wily's deception; hesitates when ordered to destroy Proto Man; maintains rivalry with Mega Man over proving superiority
Steals blueprints; invites Proto Man to join (seeking consensus); slices Proto Man when refused; questions heroes' motivation; maintains defiance after defeat; eventually seeks redemption
Arrives first at Robot Museum; refuses King's recruitment; attacks despite disadvantage; destroys King's shield; sabotages Wily's new plans; confronts Bass about lack of purpose

Remains hidden (communicates via monitor); reveals King as test of Bass's abilities; offers promises of new power; adjusts King's 'brainwashing level' to control him; never appears physically until final confrontation
Delivers letter from King to Mega Man; represents emotional continuity and hope for redemption/coexistence

notes: Mega Man & Bass communicates political ideology through character choice mechanics and narrative structure. The game's central tension is robot autonomy vs. programmed purpose: King desires robot supremacy and freedom from human rule; Mega Man fights to preserve human-robot harmony; Bass seeks individual proof of superiority; Proto Man represents true free will. Visual manifestation occurs through: (1) Boss design distinctiveness signaling threat level, (2) Environmental architecture reflecting faction philosophy, (3) Character color coding (blue hero vs. black rival), (4) Damage states indicating vulnerability despite power claims, (5) Off-screen authority (Wily on monitor) establishing hidden power structures. World-state shifts marked by sanctuary violations (Robot Museum infiltration), power transfers (blueprints stolen), and redemption arcs (King's letter). The platformer genre's level-by-level progression mirrors political 'stages' of conflict escalation.
